title: Prerenal AKI
buzzwords: kidney failure from poor perfusion
vomiting, diarrhea, or hemorrhage triggers it
orthostatic hypotension with rising creatinine
bland urinalysis with low urine sodium
BUN-to-creatinine ratio above 20
FENa under 1% from avid sodium retention
saline challenge rapidly improves GFR
NSAIDs plus ACE inhibitor classic combo

title: Acute Tubular Necrosis (ATN)
buzzwords: intrinsic AKI from tubular cell death
ischemia, sepsis, or nephrotoxin exposure
aminoglycosides, IV contrast, vancomycin, cisplatin
sloughed cells obstruct tubules with backleak
urine sodium above 40 with FENa over 2%
renal tubular epithelial cells in urine
fried-egg cells on urinalysis
muddy brown granular casts pathognomonic

title: Acute Interstitial Nephritis (AIN)
buzzwords: intrinsic AKI from drug hypersensitivity
rising creatinine after starting new medication
antibiotics, NSAIDs, or PPIs trigger it
T-cell and antibody-mediated interstitial inflammation
fever and rash with eosinophilia
WBCs and WBC casts on urinalysis
urine eosinophils on Hansel or Wright stain
FENa unreliable in this diagnosis

title: Glomerulonephritis / RPGN as Intrinsic AKI
buzzwords: intrinsic AKI from autoimmune glomerular injury
rapidly worsening renal function over weeks
proteinuria with hematuria on dipstick
dysmorphic red cells in urine sediment
Mickey Mouse ear acanthocytes in sediment
red blood cell casts on urinalysis
Goodpasture, lupus nephritis, IgA vasculitis examples
renal biopsy confirms rapidly progressive glomerulonephritis

title: Postrenal AKI (Obstruction)
buzzwords: AKI from urinary tract blockage
anuria with suprapubic fullness
older man with BPH and rising creatinine
bilateral ureteral stones or pelvic malignancy
back-pressure transmitted into Bowman space
bladder catheter both diagnoses and treats
hydronephrosis on renal ultrasound
dilated calyces with bear-paw appearance

title: AEIOU Dialysis Criteria
buzzwords: urgent indications to start renal replacement
severe metabolic derangements unresponsive to medical therapy
refractory acidosis with pH below 7.1
potassium above 6.5 despite treatment
toxic alcohol or dialyzable drug ingestion
volume overload refractory to diuretics
uremic pericarditis or encephalopathy
mnemonic AEIOU triggers urgent dialysis
